title: Why are Adam and Lillith dormant
display_title: Why are Adam and Lillith dormant
wikitext:
[]: Why are Adam and Lillith dormant?             Quote Post by Ryukin75 » Thu Jun 10, 2010 8:46 pm Why are Adam and Lillith dormant? In both series?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Jun 10, 2010 8:56 pm Because Rei and Kaworu are the vessels for the souls of Lilith and Adam, respectively. Thus, their "true" bodies would lay dormant. In Rebuild, it's not full developed yet, not to mention Adam hasn't yet been identified, although I suspect that Adam is the being on the moon.

[]:             Quote Post by Agent_Koopa » Thu Jun 10, 2010 9:15 pm Lilith's job is over. Adam is dormant because the [s]Lance[/s] Spear of Longinus impaled it after Lilith landed on Earth to prevent the two species from developing on the same planet.

[]:             Quote Post by Ryukin75 » Thu Jun 10, 2010 9:18 pm I totally agree that's Adam on the moon. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Jun 10, 2010 9:39 pm Please keep Rebuild content out of a non-Rebuild section, thank you. Anyways, neither Seed of Life possess their own soul; instead, their souls are, in a way, imprisoned in Rei and Kaworu. Lilith becomes complete after the Lance of Longinus is pulled out and Rei fuses with her. Kaworu's soul may have been floating around in Terminal Dogma after he died, and when Rei fused with Adam and Lilith, Kaworu's soul did too, thus completing both Lilith and Adam, allowing for Third Impact.

[]:             Quote Post by Podferio » Thu Jun 10, 2010 9:50 pm Sachi wrote: Kaworu's soul may have been floating around in Terminal Dogma after he died, and when Rei fused with Adam and Lilith, Kaworu's soul did too, thus completing both Lilith and Adam, allowing for Third Impact.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Thu Jun 10, 2010 9:54 pm It happened when Gendo stuck his hand in her. The fetal Adam was part of his hand.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Jun 10, 2010 9:56 pm child of Lilith wrote: It happened when Gendo stuck his hand in her. The fetal Adam was part of his hand.

[]:             Quote Post by flapjack » Thu Jun 10, 2010 11:04 pm bump

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Jun 10, 2010 11:05 pm flapjack wrote: which begs the question why or how was she dormant before the lance was placed into her.

[]:             Quote Post by The Fourth Evangelion » Fri Jun 11, 2010 5:14 pm And, after (party)crashing on a planet, a nap is a wise thing to do. I understand why Adam was so pissed that he started Second Impact...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Jun 11, 2010 5:22 pm The Fourth Evangelion wrote: I understand why Adam was so pissed that he started Second Impact...

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Fri Jun 11, 2010 5:40 pm Reichu wrote: More like Adam started Second Impact because she was pissed? Adam being dormant when discovered by the KatsExp is fully explained. Lilith sleeping through her discovery and subsequent mutilation, though? Only really explicable if she was somehow hep with her kids' need to experiment and manipulate...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Jun 11, 2010 5:51 pm Monk Ed wrote: All that stuff in Rebuild about a "contract with Lilith" makes it a lot more credible to me that NGE Lilith might have actually been actually totally cool with what they did to her.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Fri Jun 11, 2010 6:09 pm Reichu wrote: Actually been actually?

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Fri Jun 11, 2010 7:15 pm I feel like, if we're still working on OP's question, this should be moved to the Rebuild forum. NGE answered the question fairly well, so let's theorize on the other half. Monk Ed wrote: I also remind myself that Rebuild is supposed to be "more clear"

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Fri Jun 11, 2010 7:26 pm I should not have said "contract". No contract. Forget about that. What I mean is, it makes sense to me now that Lilith could have let it happen, and expected it to happen, possibly because she was 'programmed' by the FAR or had the instincts to let it happen, and perhaps the SDSS told Seele that she would let it happen. A loose, metaphorical contract, if you will. More like a natural law. I still hold it only as one of many possibilities, though. I'm not taking Rebuild any farther than that. DOOOO HO HO HO HO

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Mon Jun 14, 2010 9:16 am Monk Ed wrote: It makes sense to me now that Lilith could have let it happen, and expected it to happen, possibly because she was 'programmed' by the FAR or had the instincts to let it happen, and perhaps the SDSS told Seele that she would let it happen. A loose, metaphorical contract, if you will. More like a natural law.
